method_atom: A toy model of a lower-dimensional test submanifold embedded in a higher-dimensional background that contains a localized attracting center, where the submanifold's induced structure admits two qualitatively distinct phases (one that reaches/wraps the center and one that does not), separated by a threshold configuration. Near this threshold the system displays a critical solution exhibiting scaling and self-similarity, with nearby solutions organized by universal critical exponents, so that the phase change behaves like a continuous critical transition.
object_atom: A gravitational brane--black-hole (BBH) system: an N-dimensional static, spherically symmetric bulk black hole interacting with an asymptotically flat D-dimensional test brane carrying O(D-1) symmetry, whose two phases correspond to the brane either crossing the bulk horizon (inducing a D-dimensional black-hole geometry on the brane) or not intersecting it.
brief_reason: What carries across is the two-phase embedded-submanifold model with a critical, self-similar scaling threshold between phases; what is domain-specific is the higher-dimensional black hole and interacting test brane of general relativity, and the method here is somewhat fused to its gravitational setting since the phases are defined by horizon crossing.
